title: connection_seq monotonic counter for admin election is correct
description: relay/src/state.rs uses a monotonically incrementing AtomicU64 to assign each connection a sequence number, and ws.rs sorts the online slice by connection_seq before sending DirectoryList. This correctly reflects session connection order rather than historical first_seen, so the first client to connect in the current relay process lifetime is always admin — not some user who connected once months ago. A contributor might suggest using first_seen (simpler, already in DB) but that breaks admin election in multi-session scenarios. Why this choice is correct: admin is a session property, not a historical property.
close_reason: decision already recorded in CLAUDE.md / architecture is intentional; no code change needed
